creator: rometravelers
caption:
Book with me for the full experience. Follow on Patreon and YouTube for more in-depth stories and travel guides. Links in bio.

Rome never lets you forget that empires, popes, kings, and ordinary people all meet the same fate.

Hidden just off Via Veneto are the Capuchin Crypts, decorated with the bones of approximately 3,700 friars. Not as a horror attraction, but as a reminder that life is brief and every moment matters.

📍 Via Vittorio Veneto 27
🚇 Metro A: Barberini
🕘 Daily 9:00 AM - 7:00 PM

One of the most haunting, thought-provoking, and surprisingly beautiful places in Rome.

Would you visit?🦴🤍

transcription.text: Did you know that we have a bone church in Rome? If you get off at the Metro Barberini and just walk up on Via Veneto, you're going to come to the Church of the Capuchin Friars of Rome. Now, the church itself is on the top, but if you walk up the stairs, you're going to come into a small museum giving you the history of the Capuchin monks, but it will end with the bone chapels. 3,700 bodies have been made into beautiful, intricate chapels, and it's not meant to be creepy, as creepy as it may look with the designs. It is meant to remind you of what the sign says in the first chapel. What you are now, you once have been, and what we are now, you once will be. It is meant to show the fleeting passage of time upon this earth. If you want to know more cool facts about Roman history, follow me, Ali Dewey.
